compiled_state.title: As a developer, I can validate multi-worktree merges through E2E tests
compiled_state.author: Joe Oakhart

comments.body:
## Description

**What**: E2E test exercising two worktrees making changes and merging sequentially to main
**Why**: Multi-worktree merges are a common failure mode — ticket branch conflicts, index.json merge driver issues, and state file collisions
**Scope**:
- IN: Two worktrees with separate tickets, sequential merge to main, verify main contains both changes, verify tickets branch consistency
- OUT: Concurrent (parallel) merges — these are sequential

## Done Definitions

- When this story is complete, an E2E test creates two worktrees, each creates a ticket and makes a code change, merges the first worktree to main, then merges the second worktree, and verifies main contains both changes
  ← Satisfies: "At least one multi-worktree test"
- When this story is complete, both worktrees create at least one ticket each, and the test verifies .index.json on the tickets branch contains entries from both worktrees after both merges
  ← Satisfies: "verifying main contains both changes"
- When this story is complete, unit tests written and passing for all new or modified logic

## Considerations

- [Reliability] Ticket index merge driver (merge-ticket-index.py) must be registered via git config in Docker container before tests — worktree-sync-from-main.sh has fallback but merge-to-main.sh does not
- [Reliability] Deterministic sequencing needed — no timing-dependent operations between worktrees
comments.author: Joe Oakhart
